Tests exception handling in JavaScript functions with specific thrown values.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that verifies the order of function execution and exception handling in JavaScript. It defines two functions, [`x`](<#04-019x>) and [`y`](<#04-019y>), each of which throws a string exception, "x" and "y" respectively. The [`f_arg`](<#04-019f_arg>) function is called with the results of `x()` and `y()` as arguments within a `try` block. The script expects the [`x`](<#04-019x>) function to throw an exception first, and it uses `assert` statements to ensure that the exception handling behaves as expected. If the exception caught is "y" or anything other than "x", the script will trigger an assertion failure, indicating an unexpected behavior.
